item.title: How Apple’s big lawsuit could disrupt OpenAI’s IPO plans
item.dc:creator: Theresa Loconsolo
item.description: Apple filed a trade secrets lawsuit against OpenAI last Friday, and it’s not messing around. The complaint alleges a pattern of misconduct reaching all the way up to OpenAI’s chief hardware officer and claims more than 400 former Apple employees now work at the company. OpenAI’s response so far has been carefully hedged, and the timing couldn’t be worse with the company reportedly eyeing an IPO […]

item.title: FBI arrests man accused of using Steam games to drain victims’ crypto wallets
item.dc:creator: Lorenzo Franceschi-Bicchierai
item.description: Prosecutors accused 21-year-old student Zyaire Wilkins of publishing on Steam several fake video games that contained malware, infecting thousands of victims, and stealing crypto from some of them.

item.title: Parents want safer phones for kids. These companies are answering the call.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Forristal
item.description: As parents look for alternatives to unrestricted smartphones, a growing number of companies are building phones designed specifically for kids, from feature-limited mobile devices to minimalist home phones.

item.title: Amazon fixing bug that billed some AWS customers billions of dollars
item.dc:creator: Zack Whittaker
item.description: Some Amazon customers logged on Friday to a surprise bill estimate claiming that they owed the tech and cloud giant billions in fees.

item.title: Patreon stops asking AI bots not to scrape — and starts blocking them
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Patreon is strengthening its defenses against AI scraping by working with Cloudflare to block bots that train AI models on creators’ content without permission. The move marks a shift away from relying on websites using robots.txt alone to actively block unauthorized AI training.

item.title: reMarkable’s new Paper Pure is good. That’s why I wrote this review on it.
item.dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
item.description: Remarkable's Paper Pure replaces Remarkable 2, and it is quite good.

item.title: No product? No problem. This Disrupt 2026 session shows how to get pre-seed funding with conviction, storytelling
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch Events
item.description: It’s not just you: AI startups are taking in a huge amount of seed funding, and in the process making things harder for anyone looking for funding even at a pre-seed stage. We’ve covered the trend in detail, and at this year’s TechCrunch Disrupt event, we want to help pre-seed founders now being held to seed-stage […]

item.title: Zoox issues software recall after a robotaxi got confused by heavy smoke
item.dc:creator: Sean O'Kane
item.description: The recall comes as the top automotive safety regulator in the U.S. has warned AV companies about their vehicles interfering with first responders.

item.title: Why the first GPU financiers are turning to inference chips in a $400 million deal
item.dc:creator: Tim Fernholz
item.description: A $400 million chip-backed loan points to the next wave of AI infrastructure deals.

item.title: San Francisco mayor pushes for tougher rules after the Waymo traffic fiasco
item.dc:creator: Kirsten Korosec
item.description: In the wake of a massive hours-long gridlock event, San Francisco mayor Daniel Lurie has told state regulators it's time to put more requirements on robotaxi operators like Waymo.

item.title: SpaceX suddenly aborts second Starship V3 launch after ignition
item.dc:creator: Sean O'Kane
item.description: The company didn't immediately say what went wrong. SpaceX's stock plunged more than 4% in after-hours trading before paring losses.

item.title: Coca-Cola suspended production at its Fairlife dairy after a ransomware attack
item.dc:creator: Zack Whittaker
item.description: Coca Cola said dairy production at its Fairlife unit will "remain suspended" in the United States following a hack.

item.title: Founders Fund hires former OpenAI exec Ryan Beiermeister (and not because of her ‘Mafia’ skills)
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: Ryan Beiermeister, who demonstrated cool analysis in the Founders Fund YouTube series "Mafia," has joined the firm as a partner.

item.title: Google Vids now lets you star in your own AI videos
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Google is adding personalized AI avatars to Vids that let users create videos starring a digital version of themselves, alongside Gemini Omni-powered tools for generating and editing videos from prompts and reference images.

item.title: Roblox launches an AI-powered game-creation feature in its mobile app
item.dc:creator: Lauren Forristal
item.description: Roblox's new "Build" feature lets users generate basic games using a single text prompt.

item.title: Oil giant BP shutters its corporate venture arm after 20 years
item.dc:creator: Tim De Chant
item.description: BP Ventures is shutting down, ending a nearly 20-year run that was marked by reportedly lackluster returns.

item.title: Uber’s $14.8B Delivery Hero deal would nearly double its global footprint
item.dc:creator: Kirsten Korosec
item.description: Uber has agreed to acquire Delivery Hero in a $14.8 billion all-stock deal that would nearly double the company’s global footprint and create one of the world’s largest food-delivery platforms outside China.

item.title: Newsletter platform Beehiiv now lets subscribers chat with each other, adds AI
item.dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
item.description: Beehiiv is launching an AI Copilot to help publishers with user growth and analytics.

item.title: X cracks down on creators who steal content
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: X will use Grok AI to better detect stolen content, redirect payouts to original creators, and crack down on engagement bait.

item.title: AI-powered travel agency Fora hits unicorn status, raises $60M
item.dc:creator: Dominic-Madori Davis
item.description: Travel agency Fora announced a $60 million Series D round led by Forerunner and Tactile Ventures, valuing the company at $1 billion.
